title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
summary: Cyril Imhof compares small agile teams to large teams, arguing that smaller units achieve greater task completion while larger teams lose efficiency to excessive meetings. The view emphasizes that organizational size directly impacts productivity and meeting culture.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof contrasts small agile teams with large teams on the dimensions of productivity and meeting culture. The argument: smaller units complete more tasks, while larger teams lose efficiency to excessive meetings. The central claim is that organizational size directly shapes both output and how much time gets absorbed by meetings.
